+name: Pact Manual
+
+on:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      pact_mode:
+        description: "Pact run mode"
+        required: true
+        default: "quick"
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - quick
+          - full
+      pact_target:
+        description: "Optional Pact target. Use a spec glob like **/auth.pact.spec.ts or a grep pattern."
+        required: false
+        type: string
+      target_mode:
+        description: "Interpret pact_target as match or grep."
+        required: true
+        default: "match"
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - match
+          - grep
+
+jobs:
+  pact-manual:
+    name: Pact manual run
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version-file: .nvmrc
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install Playwright Browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps
+
+      - name: Run Pact suite manually
+        id: pact_manual
+        env:
+          LT_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.LT_USERNAME }}
+          LT_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.LT_ACCESS_KEY }}
+          E2E_TEST_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_EMAIL }}
+          E2E_TEST_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD }}
+          PACT_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: '600000'
+          PACT_TEST_MATCH: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_mode == 'match' && github.event.inputs.pact_target || '' }}
+          PACT_TEST_GREP: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_mode == 'grep' && github.event.inputs.pact_target || '' }}
+        run: |
+          if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.pact_mode }}" = "quick" ] && [ -z "${{ github.event.inputs.pact_target }}" ]; then
+            npm run test:playwright:pact:quick
+          else
+            npm run test:playwright:pact
+          fi
+
+      - name: Add manual summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Pact manual run"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.pact_mode }}"
+            echo "- Target mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_mode }}"
+            echo "- Target: ${{ github.event.inputs.pact_target || 'default' }}"
+            echo "- Result: ${{ steps.pact_manual.outcome }}"
+            echo "- Report artifact: playwright-report-manual"
+            echo "- Pact artifact: pact-files-manual"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: playwright-report-manual
+          path: playwright-report/
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-files-manual
+          path: e2e/playwright/pacts/
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30

+name: Pact Scheduled
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 6 * * *'
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+jobs:
+  pact-scheduled:
+    name: Pact scheduled/full run
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version-file: .nvmrc
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install Playwright Browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps
+
+      - name: Run full Pact suite
+        id: pact_scheduled
+        env:
+          LT_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.LT_USERNAME }}
+          LT_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.LT_ACCESS_KEY }}
+          E2E_TEST_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_EMAIL }}
+          E2E_TEST_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD }}
+          PACT_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: '600000'
+          PACT_REPORT_CHANNEL: nightly
+          PACT_REPORT_ENVIRONMENT: qa
+          GITHUB_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
+          GITHUB_REF_NAME: ${{ github.ref_name }}
+          GITHUB_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
+        run: npm run test:playwright:pact:report
+
+      - name: Build summary markdown
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+          SUMMARY_MD_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+          SUMMARY_HTML_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+        run: node ./scripts/build-summary-md.js
+
+      - name: Validate summary contract
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+        run: node ./scripts/validate-summary-contract.js
+        
+      - name: Read Pact summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        id: pact_summary
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json ]; then
+            node -e "
+              const fs = require('fs');
+              const summary = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json', 'utf8'));
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`status=\${summary.status || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`service=\${summary.service || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`lane=\${summary.lane || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`channel=\${summary.channel || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`testsRun=\${summary.testsRun ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`failures=\${summary.failures ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`errors=\${summary.errors ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`durationSeconds=\${summary.durationSeconds ?? 0}\n\`);
+            "
+          else
+            echo "status=missing" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "service=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "lane=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "channel=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "testsRun=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "failures=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "errors=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "durationSeconds=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Checkout qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          repository: JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards
+          token: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+          ref: gh-pages
+          path: qa-dashboards
+
+      - name: Publish latest nightly summary and artifacts to qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -e
+
+          TARGET_DIR="qa-dashboards/latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly"
+          ARTIFACTS_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/artifacts"
+
+          mkdir -p "$ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json "$TARGET_DIR/summary.json"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md "$TARGET_DIR/summary.md"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html "$TARGET_DIR/summary.html"
+
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/results.json ]; then
+            cp e2e/playwright/pact/results.json "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/results.json"
+          fi
+
+          if [ -d playwright-report ]; then
+            rm -rf "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+            cp -R playwright-report "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Commit and push qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        working-directory: qa-dashboards
+        shell: bash
+        env:
+          QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          node ./scripts/build-latest-index.js
+
+          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git add latest/index.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/summary.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/summary.md
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/summary.html
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/artifacts/results.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/artifacts/playwright-report
+          git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "chore: update pact nightly latest for front-customer-workspace"
+          git remote set-url origin https://x-access-token:${QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN}@github.com/JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards.git
+          git push origin HEAD:gh-pages
+
+      - name: Add scheduled summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Pact full run"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Trigger: ${{ github.event_name }}"
+            echo "- Result: ${{ steps.pact_scheduled.outcome }}"
+            echo "- Status: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.status }}"
+            echo "- Service: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.service }}"
+            echo "- Lane: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.lane }}"
+            echo "- Channel: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.channel }}"
+            echo "- Tests run: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.testsRun }}"
+            echo "- Failures: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.failures }}"
+            echo "- Errors: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.errors }}"
+            echo "- Duration (s): ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.durationSeconds }}"
+            echo "- Report artifact: playwright-report-full"
+            echo "- Pact artifact: pact-files-full"
+            echo "- Summary artifact: pact-summary-full"
+            echo "- Mirror path: latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: playwright-report-full
+          path: playwright-report/
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-files-full
+          path: e2e/playwright/pacts/
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-summary-full
+          path: |
+            e2e/playwright/pact/results.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30

+name: Pact PR Signal
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main, master, uat]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+jobs:
+  pact-pr-signal:
+    name: Pact PR signal (non-blocking)
+    continue-on-error: true
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version-file: .nvmrc
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install Playwright Browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps
+
+      - name: Run Pact suite for PR signal
+        id: pact_pr
+        continue-on-error: true
+        env:
+          LT_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.LT_USERNAME }}
+          LT_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.LT_ACCESS_KEY }}
+          E2E_TEST_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_EMAIL }}
+          E2E_TEST_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD }}
+          PACT_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: '600000'
+          PACT_REPORT_CHANNEL: pr
+          PACT_REPORT_ENVIRONMENT: qa
+          GITHUB_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
+          GITHUB_REF_NAME: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
+          GITHUB_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
+        run: |
+          npm run test:playwright:pact:quick
+          npm run test:playwright:pact:summary
+
+      - name: Build summary markdown
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+          SUMMARY_MD_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+          SUMMARY_HTML_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+        run: node ./scripts/build-summary-md.js
+
+      - name: Validate summary contract
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+        run: node ./scripts/validate-summary-contract.js
+
+      - name: Read Pact summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        id: pact_summary
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json ]; then
+            node -e "
+              const fs = require('fs');
+              const summary = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json', 'utf8'));
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`status=\${summary.status || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`service=\${summary.service || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`lane=\${summary.lane || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`channel=\${summary.channel || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`testsRun=\${summary.testsRun ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`failures=\${summary.failures ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`errors=\${summary.errors ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`durationSeconds=\${summary.durationSeconds ?? 0}\n\`);
+            "
+          else
+            echo "status=missing" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "service=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "lane=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "channel=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "testsRun=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "failures=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "errors=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "durationSeconds=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Checkout qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          repository: JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards
+          token: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+          ref: gh-pages
+          path: qa-dashboards
+
+      - name: Publish latest PR summary and artifacts to qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -e
+
+          TARGET_DIR="qa-dashboards/latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr"
+          ARTIFACTS_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/artifacts"
+
+          mkdir -p "$ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json "$TARGET_DIR/summary.json"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md "$TARGET_DIR/summary.md"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html "$TARGET_DIR/summary.html"
+
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/results.json ]; then
+            cp e2e/playwright/pact/results.json "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/results.json"
+          fi
+
+          if [ -d playwright-report ]; then
+            rm -rf "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+            cp -R playwright-report "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Commit and push qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        working-directory: qa-dashboards

+        env:
+          QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+        run: |

+

+          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/summary.md

+          git push origin HEAD:gh-pages
+

+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Pact PR signal"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Blocking: no"
+            echo "- Mode: quick (auth contracts)"
+            echo "- Result: ${{ steps.pact_pr.outcome }}"
+            echo "- Status: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.status }}"
+            echo "- Service: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.service }}"
+            echo "- Lane: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.lane }}"
+            echo "- Channel: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.channel }}"
+            echo "- Tests run: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.testsRun }}"
+            echo "- Failures: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.failures }}"
+            echo "- Errors: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.errors }}"
+            echo "- Duration (s): ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.durationSeconds }}"
+            echo "- Report artifact: playwright-report-pr"
+            echo "- Pact artifact: pact-files-pr"
+            echo "- Summary artifact: pact-summary-pr"
+            echo "- Mirror path: latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: playwright-report-pr
+          path: playwright-report/
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-files-pr
+          path: e2e/playwright/pacts/
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-summary-pr
+          path: |
+            e2e/playwright/pact/results.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30

+

+- On pull requests, Pact runs as a non-blocking signal so the team can monitor stability before turning it into a hard gate.
+- A manual GitHub Actions flow is available with `quick` and `full` modes for ad-hoc Pact validation.

+  className?: string
+  isNotificationsEnabled?: boolean
+}
+
+export default function AppleButton({
+  isSignup = false,
+  onPress,
+  disabled = false,
+  className = '',
+  isNotificationsEnabled,
+}: AppleButtonProps) {
+  const { redirectToAppleOAuth, isGoogleOAuthLoading } = useAuth()
+  const { translations } = useLanguage()
+  const { trackEvent } = useTracking()
+  const { activePromotion } = usePromotions()
+  const { hasFeatureFlag } = useFeatureFlags()
+
+  const handlePress = async () => {
+    if (onPress) {
+      onPress()
+      return
+    }
+
+    try {
+      if (isSignup) {
+        trackEvent(TRACKING_EVENTS.CLICK_REGISTER_SUCCESS)
+      } else {
+        trackEvent(TRACKING_EVENTS.START_LOGIN)
+      }
+
+      await redirectToAppleOAuth(
+        isSignup,
+        isSignup ? activePromotion : undefined,
+        isSignup ? isNotificationsEnabled : undefined,
+      )
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error('Apple OAuth error:', error)
+    }
+  }
+
+  const buttonText = isSignup
+    ? translations['SIGNUP_CONTINUE_WITH_APPLE'] || 'Continuar con Apple'
+    : translations['LOGIN_CONTINUE_WITH_APPLE'] || 'Continuar con Apple'
+
+  if (!hasFeatureFlag(FeatureFlags.FEATURE_APPLE_OAUTH)) {
+    return null
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <Pressable
+      onPress={handlePress}
+      disabled={disabled || isGoogleOAuthLoading}
+      className={`
+        flex
+        flex-row
+        items-center
+        justify-center
+        px-8
+        py-1
+        h-12
+        bg-white
+        border
+        border-gray-400
+        rounded
+        rounded-[100px]
+        self-stretch
+        order-1
+        gap-2
+        active:opacity-60
+        ${disabled || isGoogleOAuthLoading ? 'opacity-50' : ''}
+        ${className}
+      `}
+      style={({ pressed }) => ({
+        opacity: pressed ? 0.6 : 1,
+      })}
+      testID={isSignup ? 'apple-signup-button' : 'apple-signin-button'}
+      accessibilityLabel={buttonText}
+    >
+      <AppleIcon width={24} height={24} />
+      <Typography variant='subtitle' weight='bold'>
+        {isGoogleOAuthLoading
+          ? translations['LOADING'] || 'Cargando...'
+          : buttonText}
+      </Typography>
+    </Pressable>
+  )
+}

+import { formatDate } from '../useFormattedDate'
+
+describe('formatDate (extra coverage)', () => {
+  it('returns only time for time format', () => {
+    const value = formatDate('2024-06-10T09:45:00Z', 'time', 'en-GB')
+    expect(value).toMatch(/\d{2}:\d{2}/)
+    expect(value).toContain(':45')
+  })
+
+  it('adaptiveTime returns time for same day', () => {
+    const now = new Date()
+    const sameDayIso = new Date(
+      now.getFullYear(),
+      now.getMonth(),
+      now.getDate(),
+      11,
+      30,
+      0,
+    ).toISOString()
+
+    const value = formatDate(sameDayIso, 'adaptiveTime', 'en-GB')
+    expect(value).toMatch(/\d{2}:\d{2}/)
+  })
+
+  it('adaptiveTime returns date + time when date differs from today', () => {
+    const value = formatDate('2020-01-01T08:00:00Z', 'adaptiveTime', 'en-GB')
+    expect(value).toContain('01/01/2020')
+  })
+
+  it('returns empty string for falsy values', () => {
+    expect(formatDate(null, 'date')).toBe('')
+    expect(formatDate(undefined, 'datetime')).toBe('')
+  })
+})

+import { useContext } from 'react'
+import { usePromotions } from '../api/promotions/usePromotions'
+import { promotionsApi } from '@/app/services/api/promotions'
+import { toast } from '@/app/services/shared/toast'
+
+jest.mock('react', () => ({
+  ...jest.requireActual('react'),
+  useContext: jest.fn(),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/api/promotions', () => ({
+  promotionsApi: {
+    getWidget: jest.fn(),
+    validateHash: jest.fn(),
+    updateCard: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/shared/toast', () => ({
+  toast: { show: jest.fn() },
+}))
+
+const mockUseContext = useContext as jest.Mock
+const mockGetWidget = promotionsApi.getWidget as jest.Mock
+const mockValidateHash = promotionsApi.validateHash as jest.Mock
+const mockUpdateCard = promotionsApi.updateCard as jest.Mock
+
+const mockContext = {
+  hasPromotion: jest.fn(),
+  deletePromotion: jest.fn(),
+  activePromotion: 'mastercard' as const,
+  isLoading: false,
+}
+
+beforeEach(() => {
+  jest.clearAllMocks()
+  mockUseContext.mockReturnValue(mockContext)
+})
+
+describe('usePromotions', () => {
+  describe('context validation', () => {
+    it('throws when used outside PromotionsProvider', () => {
+      mockUseContext.mockReturnValue(undefined)
+      expect(() => usePromotions()).toThrow(
+        'usePromotions must be used within a PromotionsProvider',
+      )
+    })
+
+    it('returns context values', () => {
+      const { activePromotion, hasPromotion, deletePromotion } =
+        usePromotions()
+      expect(activePromotion).toBe('mastercard')
+      expect(hasPromotion).toBe(mockContext.hasPromotion)
+      expect(deletePromotion).toBe(mockContext.deletePromotion)
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('getWidget', () => {
+    it('returns widget data on success', async () => {
+      const widgetData = {
+        accessToken: 'token-123',
+        url: 'https://example.com',
+        vendorCode: 'VC01',
+      }
+      mockGetWidget.mockResolvedValue(widgetData)
+
+      const { getWidget } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await getWidget()
+
+      expect(result).toEqual(widgetData)
+      expect(mockGetWidget).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
+    })
+
+    it('returns null and shows toast when API returns falsy', async () => {
+      mockGetWidget.mockResolvedValue(null)
+
+      const { getWidget } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await getWidget()
+
+      expect(result).toBeNull()
+      expect(toast.show).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        title: 'Error fetching promotion widget',
+        type: 'error',
+      })
+    })
+
+    it('returns null and shows toast on API error', async () => {
+      mockGetWidget.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Network error'))
+
+      const { getWidget } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await getWidget()
+
+      expect(result).toBeNull()
+      expect(toast.show).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        title: 'Error fetching promotion widget',
+        type: 'error',
+      })
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('validateCardNumberId', () => {
+    it('returns validation result on success', async () => {
+      const validResponse = { valid: true }
+      mockValidateHash.mockResolvedValue(validResponse)
+
+      const { validateCardNumberId } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await validateCardNumberId('card-abc')
+
+      expect(result).toEqual(validResponse)
+      expect(mockValidateHash).toHaveBeenCalledWith('card-abc')
+    })
+
+    it('returns invalid with error on API failure', async () => {
+      mockValidateHash.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error'))
+
+      const { validateCardNumberId } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await validateCardNumberId('card-abc')
+
+      expect(result).toEqual({
+        valid: false,
+        errors: [{ msg: 'Validation failed due to a nunknown error' }],
+      })
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('updateCard', () => {
+    it('calls API with card identifier', async () => {
+      mockUpdateCard.mockResolvedValue(undefined)
+
+      const { updateCard } = usePromotions()
+      await updateCard('card-xyz')
+
+      expect(mockUpdateCard).toHaveBeenCalledWith('card-xyz')
+    })
+
+    it('rethrows on API error', async () => {
+      const error = new Error('Update failed')
+      mockUpdateCard.mockRejectedValue(error)
+
+      const { updateCard } = usePromotions()
+      await expect(updateCard('card-xyz')).rejects.toThrow('Update failed')
+    })
+  })
+})

+import { renderHook, act } from '@testing-library/react'

+}))
+

+  NavigationManager: {
+    replace: jest.fn(),
+    navigate: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useRouteRedirection (extra coverage)', () => {
+  const useGeneralConfigMock =
+    require('@/app/providers/GeneralConfigProvider')
+      .useGeneralConfig as jest.Mock
+  const useCommercesMock =
+    require('@/app/hooks/api/commerces/useCommerces').useCommerces as jest.Mock
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    useGeneralConfigMock.mockReturnValue({
+      config: {
+        contextBarMerchantSelected: 'company-1:commerce-1',
+      },
+    })
+    useCommercesMock.mockReturnValue({
+      commerce: {
+        id: 'commerce-2',
+        companyId: 'company-2',
+      },
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('navigates to dynamic route with query params when resolvable', async () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    let response = false
+    await act(async () => {
+      response = await result.current.navigateToDynamicRoute('/l', {
+        source: 'test',
+      })
+    })
+
+    expect(response).toBe(true)
+    expect(NavigationManager.replace).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      '/(drawer)/(tabs)/company/company-1/commerce/commerce-1/presencia-digital/datos-de-publicacion?source=test',
+    )
+  })
+
+  it('falls back when required params cannot be resolved', async () => {
+    useGeneralConfigMock.mockReturnValue({
+      config: {
+        contextBarMerchantSelected: '',
+      },
+    })
+    useCommercesMock.mockReturnValue({
+      commerce: undefined,
+    })
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    let response = true
+    await act(async () => {
+      response = await result.current.navigateToDynamicRoute('/reviews')
+    })
+
+    expect(response).toBe(false)
+    expect(NavigationManager.replace).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/')
+  })
+
+  it('returns false when mapping does not exist', async () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    let response = true
+    await act(async () => {
+      response = await result.current.navigateToDynamicRoute('/does-not-exist')
+    })
+
+    expect(response).toBe(false)
+    expect(NavigationManager.replace).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('canResolveRoute returns false when params are unavailable', () => {
+    useGeneralConfigMock.mockReturnValue({
+      config: {
+        contextBarMerchantSelected: '',
+      },
+    })
+    useCommercesMock.mockReturnValue({
+      commerce: undefined,
+    })
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    expect(result.current.canResolveRoute('/l')).toBe(false)
+  })
+})

+import { renderHook } from '@testing-library/react-native'
+import useSWR from 'swr'
+
+import { useBilling } from './useBilling'
+import { billingApi } from '../../../services/api/billing'
+
+jest.mock('swr')
+
+jest.mock('../../../services/api/billing', () => ({
+  billingApi: {
+    getBilling: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useBilling (extra coverage)', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    ;(useSWR as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      data: [],
+      error: undefined,
+      isLoading: false,
+      mutate: jest.fn(),
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('calls billing API for each valid merchant id in fetcher', async () => {
+    ;(billingApi.getBilling as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue([{ invoiceId: '1' }])
+
+    renderHook(() =>
+      useBilling('2024-01-01', '2024-01-31', 1, 10, [
+        'merchant-1',
+        undefined,
+        'merchant-2',
+      ]),
+    )
+
+    const fetcher = (useSWR as jest.Mock).mock.calls[0][1]
+    await fetcher()
+
+    expect(billingApi.getBilling).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)
+    expect(billingApi.getBilling).toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(
+      1,
+      '2024-01-01',
+      '2024-01-31',
+      'merchant-1',
+      1,
+      10,
+    )
+    expect(billingApi.getBilling).toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(
+      2,
+      '2024-01-01',
+      '2024-01-31',
+      'merchant-2',
+      1,
+      10,
+    )
+  })
+})

+import { renderHook, act } from '@testing-library/react-native'
+import useSWR from 'swr'
+
+import { useCommerces } from './useCommerces'

+
+jest.mock('swr')
+

+  useGlobalSearchParams: jest.fn(() => ({
+    commerce_id: 'commerce-2',
+  })),
+}))
+

+  useAuth: jest.fn(() => ({
+    profile: { id: 'user-1' },
+  })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/providers/LanguageProvider', () => ({
+  useLanguage: jest.fn(() => ({ selectedLang: 'es-ES' })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('../translations/useTranslations', () => ({
+  useTranslations: jest.fn(() => ({
+    translations: {
+      ERROR_UPDATING_PLACES: 'Error updating places',
+    },
+  })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/providers/GeneralConfigProvider', () => ({
+  useGeneralConfig: jest.fn(() => ({
+    config: {
+      contextBarMerchantSelected: 'company-1:commerce-1',
+    },
+  })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/api/commerces/api', () => ({
+  commerceApi: {
+    getCommerces: jest.fn(),
+    updateCommerce: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/shared/toast', () => ({
+  toast: {
+    show: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useCommerces updateCommerce', () => {
+  const mutate = jest.fn()
+
+  const swrData = {
+    commerces: [
+      { id: 'commerce-1', companyId: 'company-1', name: 'One' },
+      { id: 'commerce-2', companyId: 'company-1', name: 'Two' },
+    ],
+    companies: [{ id: 'company-1', name: 'Company' }],
+  }
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    ;(useSWR as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      data: swrData,
+      error: undefined,
+      isLoading: false,
+      mutate,
+    })
+    mutate.mockResolvedValue(swrData)
+    ;(commerceApi.updateCommerce as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(undefined)
+  })
+
+  it('computes selectedMerchant and commerce from context/search params', () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    expect(result.current.selectedMerchant?.id).toBe('commerce-1')
+    expect(result.current.commerce?.id).toBe('commerce-2')
+  })
+
+  it('updates commerce with object payload using optimistic update + revalidate', async () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.updateCommerce('commerce-1', { name: 'Updated' })
+    })
+
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function), false)
+    expect(commerceApi.updateCommerce).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      'commerce-1',
+      'merchant',
+      { name: 'Updated' },
+    )
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)
+  })
+
+  it('updates commerce with FormData and executes onSuccess callback', async () => {
+    const onSuccess = jest.fn()
+    const formData = new FormData()
+    formData.append('file', 'avatar')
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.updateCommerce('commerce-2', formData, onSuccess)
+    })
+
+    expect(commerceApi.updateCommerce).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      'commerce-2',
+      'merchant',
+      formData,
+    )
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
+    expect(onSuccess).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
+  })
+
+  it('restores previous data and shows toast when update fails', async () => {
+    ;(commerceApi.updateCommerce as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(
+      new Error('boom'),
+    )
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.updateCommerce('commerce-1', { name: 'Broken' })
+    })
+
+    expect(toast.show).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+      title: 'Error updating places',
+      type: 'error',
+    })
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledWith(swrData, false)
+  })
+})

--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/workflows/pact-manual.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,92 @@
+name: Pact Manual
+
+on:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      pact_mode:
+        description: "Pact run mode"
+        required: true
+        default: "quick"
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - quick
+          - full
+      pact_target:
+        description: "Optional Pact target. Use a spec glob like **/auth.pact.spec.ts or a grep pattern."
+        required: false
+        type: string
+      target_mode:
+        description: "Interpret pact_target as match or grep."
+        required: true
+        default: "match"
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - match
+          - grep
+
+jobs:
+  pact-manual:
+    name: Pact manual run
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version-file: .nvmrc
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install Playwright Browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps
+
+      - name: Run Pact suite manually
+        id: pact_manual
+        env:
+          LT_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.LT_USERNAME }}
+          LT_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.LT_ACCESS_KEY }}
+          E2E_TEST_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_EMAIL }}
+          E2E_TEST_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD }}
+          PACT_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: '600000'
+          PACT_TEST_MATCH: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_mode == 'match' && github.event.inputs.pact_target || '' }}
+          PACT_TEST_GREP: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_mode == 'grep' && github.event.inputs.pact_target || '' }}
+        run: |
+          if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.pact_mode }}" = "quick" ] && [ -z "${{ github.event.inputs.pact_target }}" ]; then
+            npm run test:playwright:pact:quick
+          else
+            npm run test:playwright:pact
+          fi
+
+      - name: Add manual summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Pact manual run"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.pact_mode }}"
+            echo "- Target mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.target_mode }}"
+            echo "- Target: ${{ github.event.inputs.pact_target || 'default' }}"
+            echo "- Result: ${{ steps.pact_manual.outcome }}"
+            echo "- Report artifact: playwright-report-manual"
+            echo "- Pact artifact: pact-files-manual"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: playwright-report-manual
+          path: playwright-report/
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-files-manual
+          path: e2e/playwright/pacts/
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30

@@ -0,0 +1,188 @@
+name: Pact Scheduled
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 6 * * *'
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+jobs:
+  pact-scheduled:
+    name: Pact scheduled/full run
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version-file: .nvmrc
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install Playwright Browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps
+
+      - name: Run full Pact suite
+        id: pact_scheduled
+        env:
+          LT_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.LT_USERNAME }}
+          LT_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.LT_ACCESS_KEY }}
+          E2E_TEST_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_EMAIL }}
+          E2E_TEST_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD }}
+          PACT_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: '600000'
+          PACT_REPORT_CHANNEL: nightly
+          PACT_REPORT_ENVIRONMENT: qa
+          GITHUB_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
+          GITHUB_REF_NAME: ${{ github.ref_name }}
+          GITHUB_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
+        run: npm run test:playwright:pact:report
+
+      - name: Build summary markdown
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+          SUMMARY_MD_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+          SUMMARY_HTML_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+        run: node ./scripts/build-summary-md.js
+
+      - name: Validate summary contract
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+        run: node ./scripts/validate-summary-contract.js
+        
+      - name: Read Pact summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        id: pact_summary
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json ]; then
+            node -e "
+              const fs = require('fs');
+              const summary = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json', 'utf8'));
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`status=\${summary.status || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`service=\${summary.service || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`lane=\${summary.lane || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`channel=\${summary.channel || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`testsRun=\${summary.testsRun ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`failures=\${summary.failures ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`errors=\${summary.errors ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`durationSeconds=\${summary.durationSeconds ?? 0}\n\`);
+            "
+          else
+            echo "status=missing" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "service=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "lane=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "channel=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "testsRun=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "failures=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "errors=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "durationSeconds=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Checkout qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          repository: JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards
+          token: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+          ref: gh-pages
+          path: qa-dashboards
+
+      - name: Publish latest nightly summary and artifacts to qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -e
+
+          TARGET_DIR="qa-dashboards/latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly"
+          ARTIFACTS_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/artifacts"
+
+          mkdir -p "$ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json "$TARGET_DIR/summary.json"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md "$TARGET_DIR/summary.md"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html "$TARGET_DIR/summary.html"
+
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/results.json ]; then
+            cp e2e/playwright/pact/results.json "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/results.json"
+          fi
+
+          if [ -d playwright-report ]; then
+            rm -rf "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+            cp -R playwright-report "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Commit and push qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        working-directory: qa-dashboards
+        shell: bash
+        env:
+          QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          node ./scripts/build-latest-index.js
+
+          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git add latest/index.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/summary.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/summary.md
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/summary.html
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/artifacts/results.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/artifacts/playwright-report
+          git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "chore: update pact nightly latest for front-customer-workspace"
+          git remote set-url origin https://x-access-token:${QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN}@github.com/JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards.git
+          git push origin HEAD:gh-pages
+
+      - name: Add scheduled summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Pact full run"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Trigger: ${{ github.event_name }}"
+            echo "- Result: ${{ steps.pact_scheduled.outcome }}"
+            echo "- Status: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.status }}"
+            echo "- Service: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.service }}"
+            echo "- Lane: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.lane }}"
+            echo "- Channel: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.channel }}"
+            echo "- Tests run: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.testsRun }}"
+            echo "- Failures: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.failures }}"
+            echo "- Errors: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.errors }}"
+            echo "- Duration (s): ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.durationSeconds }}"
+            echo "- Report artifact: playwright-report-full"
+            echo "- Pact artifact: pact-files-full"
+            echo "- Summary artifact: pact-summary-full"
+            echo "- Mirror path: latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/nightly/"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: playwright-report-full
+          path: playwright-report/
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-files-full
+          path: e2e/playwright/pacts/
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-summary-full
+          path: |
+            e2e/playwright/pact/results.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30

+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main, master, uat]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+jobs:
+  pact-pr-signal:
+    name: Pact PR signal (non-blocking)
+    continue-on-error: true
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version-file: .nvmrc
+          cache: npm
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Install Playwright Browsers
+        run: npx playwright install --with-deps
+
+      - name: Run Pact suite for PR signal
+        id: pact_pr
+        continue-on-error: true
+        env:
+          LT_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.LT_USERNAME }}
+          LT_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.LT_ACCESS_KEY }}
+          E2E_TEST_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_EMAIL }}
+          E2E_TEST_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.E2E_TEST_PASSWORD }}
+          PACT_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: '600000'
+          PACT_REPORT_CHANNEL: pr
+          PACT_REPORT_ENVIRONMENT: qa
+          GITHUB_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
+          GITHUB_REF_NAME: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
+          GITHUB_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
+        run: |
+          npm run test:playwright:pact:quick
+          npm run test:playwright:pact:summary
+
+      - name: Build summary markdown
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+          SUMMARY_MD_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+          SUMMARY_HTML_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+        run: node ./scripts/build-summary-md.js
+
+      - name: Validate summary contract
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        env:
+          SUMMARY_JSON_PATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+        run: node ./scripts/validate-summary-contract.js
+
+      - name: Read Pact summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        id: pact_summary
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json ]; then
+            node -e "
+              const fs = require('fs');
+              const summary = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json', 'utf8'));
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`status=\${summary.status || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`service=\${summary.service || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`lane=\${summary.lane || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`channel=\${summary.channel || 'unknown'}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`testsRun=\${summary.testsRun ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`failures=\${summary.failures ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`errors=\${summary.errors ?? 0}\n\`);
+              fs.appendFileSync(process.env.GITHUB_OUTPUT, \`durationSeconds=\${summary.durationSeconds ?? 0}\n\`);
+            "
+          else
+            echo "status=missing" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "service=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "lane=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "channel=unknown" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "testsRun=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "failures=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "errors=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+            echo "durationSeconds=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Checkout qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          repository: JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards
+          token: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+          ref: gh-pages
+          path: qa-dashboards
+
+      - name: Publish latest PR summary and artifacts to qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          set -e
+
+          TARGET_DIR="qa-dashboards/latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr"
+          ARTIFACTS_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/artifacts"
+
+          mkdir -p "$ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json "$TARGET_DIR/summary.json"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md "$TARGET_DIR/summary.md"
+          cp e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html "$TARGET_DIR/summary.html"
+
+          if [ -f e2e/playwright/pact/results.json ]; then
+            cp e2e/playwright/pact/results.json "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/results.json"
+          fi
+
+          if [ -d playwright-report ]; then
+            rm -rf "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+            cp -R playwright-report "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/playwright-report"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Commit and push qa-dashboards
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        working-directory: qa-dashboards
+        shell: bash
+        env:
+          QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          node ./scripts/build-latest-index.js
+
+          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git add latest/index.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/summary.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/summary.md
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/summary.html
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/artifacts/results.json
+          git add latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/artifacts/playwright-report
+          git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "chore: update pact pr latest for front-customer-workspace"
+          git remote set-url origin https://x-access-token:${QA_DASHBOARDS_PUSH_TOKEN}@github.com/JavierNaranjo-BD/qa-dashboards.git
+          git push origin HEAD:gh-pages
+
+      - name: Add PR summary
+        if: ${{ always() }}
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          {
+            echo "## Pact PR signal"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Blocking: no"
+            echo "- Mode: quick (auth contracts)"
+            echo "- Result: ${{ steps.pact_pr.outcome }}"
+            echo "- Status: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.status }}"
+            echo "- Service: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.service }}"
+            echo "- Lane: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.lane }}"
+            echo "- Channel: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.channel }}"
+            echo "- Tests run: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.testsRun }}"
+            echo "- Failures: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.failures }}"
+            echo "- Errors: ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.errors }}"
+            echo "- Duration (s): ${{ steps.pact_summary.outputs.durationSeconds }}"
+            echo "- Report artifact: playwright-report-pr"
+            echo "- Pact artifact: pact-files-pr"
+            echo "- Summary artifact: pact-summary-pr"
+            echo "- Mirror path: latest/front-customer-workspace/pact/pr/"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: playwright-report-pr
+          path: playwright-report/
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-files-pr
+          path: e2e/playwright/pacts/
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30
+
+      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
+        with:
+          name: pact-summary-pr
+          path: |
+            e2e/playwright/pact/results.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.json
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.md
+            e2e/playwright/pact/summary.html
+          if-no-files-found: warn
+          retention-days: 30

 This suite uses `playwright.pact.config.ts` and routes all backend calls to a Pact mock server (`http://localhost:4015/api/v1`), with captcha bypass enabled only for e2e.
 
+#### Pact suite scope and prerequisites
+
+- Scope: consumer contract coverage for the auth flows exercised in `e2e/playwright/specs/*.pact.spec.ts`.
+- Current scope does not include broker publication or provider verification.
+- Runtime: Node `20.x`, pinned to `20.18.1` in `.nvmrc` and `.node-version` for local setup and CI.
+- Install strategy: `npm ci`.
+- Pact files are written to `e2e/playwright/pacts/`.
+
+Recommended local setup before running Pact:
+
+```bash
+npm ci
+npm run test:playwright:pact
+```
+
+Quick local run for a fast signal:
+
+```bash
+npm run test:playwright:pact:quick
+```
+
+Optional targeting for ad-hoc local runs:
+
+```powershell
+$env:PACT_TEST_MATCH='**/auth.pact.spec.ts'; npm run test:playwright:pact
+$env:PACT_TEST_GREP='mastercard'; npm run test:playwright:pact
+```
+
+Notes about supported environments:
+
+- CI runs the Pact suite on Linux with the pinned Node version.
+- On Windows, the suite also requires that the host allows loading native `.node` addons used by Pact.
+- If the host blocks native addons, the preflight now stops early with an actionable message instead of failing later with `pactffiInitWithLogLevel`.
+- On pull requests, Pact runs as a non-blocking signal so the team can monitor stability before turning it into a hard gate.
+- A manual GitHub Actions flow is available with `quick` and `full` modes for ad-hoc Pact validation.
+
+Evidence to attach to QA-9 when closing the work:
+
+- executed command
+- result summary
+- workflow/report link
+- pact file output path
+- known risks or follow-up tickets
+

diff --git a/app/components/shared/AppleButton/AppleButton.tsx b/app/components/shared/AppleButton/AppleButton.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f703195
--- /dev/null
+++ b/app/components/shared/AppleButton/AppleButton.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,103 @@
+import React from 'react'
+import { Pressable } from 'react-native'
+import { Typography } from '../Typography'
+import AppleIcon from '../../../../assets/icons/apple.svg'
+import { useAuth } from '@/app/hooks/api/auth/useAuth'
+import { useLanguage } from '@/app/providers/LanguageProvider'
+import { useTracking } from '@/app/hooks/useTracking'
+import { TRACKING_EVENTS } from '@/app/config/analytics/tracking-events'
+import { usePromotions } from '@/app/hooks/api/promotions/usePromotions'
+import { useFeatureFlags } from '@/app/hooks/api/config/useConfig'
+import { FeatureFlags } from '@/app/types/featureFlags'
+
+interface AppleButtonProps {
+  isSignup?: boolean
+  onPress?: () => void
+  disabled?: boolean
+  className?: string
+  isNotificationsEnabled?: boolean
+}
+
+export default function AppleButton({
+  isSignup = false,
+  onPress,
+  disabled = false,
+  className = '',
+  isNotificationsEnabled,
+}: AppleButtonProps) {
+  const { redirectToAppleOAuth, isGoogleOAuthLoading } = useAuth()
+  const { translations } = useLanguage()
+  const { trackEvent } = useTracking()
+  const { activePromotion } = usePromotions()
+  const { hasFeatureFlag } = useFeatureFlags()
+
+  const handlePress = async () => {
+    if (onPress) {
+      onPress()
+      return
+    }
+
+    try {
+      if (isSignup) {
+        trackEvent(TRACKING_EVENTS.CLICK_REGISTER_SUCCESS)
+      } else {
+        trackEvent(TRACKING_EVENTS.START_LOGIN)
+      }
+
+      await redirectToAppleOAuth(
+        isSignup,
+        isSignup ? activePromotion : undefined,
+        isSignup ? isNotificationsEnabled : undefined,
+      )
+    } catch (error) {
+      console.error('Apple OAuth error:', error)
+    }
+  }
+
+  const buttonText = isSignup
+    ? translations['SIGNUP_CONTINUE_WITH_APPLE'] || 'Continuar con Apple'
+    : translations['LOGIN_CONTINUE_WITH_APPLE'] || 'Continuar con Apple'
+
+  if (!hasFeatureFlag(FeatureFlags.FEATURE_APPLE_OAUTH)) {
+    return null
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <Pressable
+      onPress={handlePress}
+      disabled={disabled || isGoogleOAuthLoading}
+      className={`
+        flex
+        flex-row
+        items-center
+        justify-center
+        px-8
+        py-1
+        h-12
+        bg-white
+        border
+        border-gray-400
+        rounded
+        rounded-[100px]
+        self-stretch
+        order-1
+        gap-2
+        active:opacity-60
+        ${disabled || isGoogleOAuthLoading ? 'opacity-50' : ''}
+        ${className}
+      `}
+      style={({ pressed }) => ({
+        opacity: pressed ? 0.6 : 1,
+      })}
+      testID={isSignup ? 'apple-signup-button' : 'apple-signin-button'}
+      accessibilityLabel={buttonText}
+    >
+      <AppleIcon width={24} height={24} />
+      <Typography variant='subtitle' weight='bold'>
+        {isGoogleOAuthLoading
+          ? translations['LOADING'] || 'Cargando...'
+          : buttonText}
+      </Typography>
+    </Pressable>
+  )
+}

+import { useContext } from 'react'
+import { usePromotions } from '../api/promotions/usePromotions'
+import { promotionsApi } from '@/app/services/api/promotions'
+import { toast } from '@/app/services/shared/toast'
+
+jest.mock('react', () => ({

+  useContext: jest.fn(),
+}))
+

+  promotionsApi: {
+    getWidget: jest.fn(),
+    validateHash: jest.fn(),
+    updateCard: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/shared/toast', () => ({
+  toast: { show: jest.fn() },
+}))
+
+const mockUseContext = useContext as jest.Mock
+const mockGetWidget = promotionsApi.getWidget as jest.Mock
+const mockValidateHash = promotionsApi.validateHash as jest.Mock
+const mockUpdateCard = promotionsApi.updateCard as jest.Mock
+
+const mockContext = {
+  hasPromotion: jest.fn(),
+  deletePromotion: jest.fn(),
+  activePromotion: 'mastercard' as const,
+  isLoading: false,
+}
+
+beforeEach(() => {
+  jest.clearAllMocks()
+  mockUseContext.mockReturnValue(mockContext)
+})
+
+describe('usePromotions', () => {
+  describe('context validation', () => {
+    it('throws when used outside PromotionsProvider', () => {
+      mockUseContext.mockReturnValue(undefined)
+      expect(() => usePromotions()).toThrow(
+        'usePromotions must be used within a PromotionsProvider',
+      )
+    })
+
+    it('returns context values', () => {
+      const { activePromotion, hasPromotion, deletePromotion } =
+        usePromotions()
+      expect(activePromotion).toBe('mastercard')
+      expect(hasPromotion).toBe(mockContext.hasPromotion)
+      expect(deletePromotion).toBe(mockContext.deletePromotion)
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('getWidget', () => {
+    it('returns widget data on success', async () => {
+      const widgetData = {
+        accessToken: 'token-123',
+        url: 'https://example.com',
+        vendorCode: 'VC01',
+      }
+      mockGetWidget.mockResolvedValue(widgetData)
+
+      const { getWidget } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await getWidget()
+
+      expect(result).toEqual(widgetData)
+      expect(mockGetWidget).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
+    })
+
+    it('returns null and shows toast when API returns falsy', async () => {
+      mockGetWidget.mockResolvedValue(null)
+
+      const { getWidget } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await getWidget()
+
+      expect(result).toBeNull()
+      expect(toast.show).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        title: 'Error fetching promotion widget',
+        type: 'error',
+      })
+    })
+
+    it('returns null and shows toast on API error', async () => {
+      mockGetWidget.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Network error'))
+
+      const { getWidget } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await getWidget()
+
+      expect(result).toBeNull()
+      expect(toast.show).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+        title: 'Error fetching promotion widget',
+        type: 'error',
+      })
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('validateCardNumberId', () => {
+    it('returns validation result on success', async () => {
+      const validResponse = { valid: true }
+      mockValidateHash.mockResolvedValue(validResponse)
+
+      const { validateCardNumberId } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await validateCardNumberId('card-abc')
+
+      expect(result).toEqual(validResponse)
+      expect(mockValidateHash).toHaveBeenCalledWith('card-abc')
+    })
+
+    it('returns invalid with error on API failure', async () => {
+      mockValidateHash.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error'))
+
+      const { validateCardNumberId } = usePromotions()
+      const result = await validateCardNumberId('card-abc')
+
+      expect(result).toEqual({
+        valid: false,
+        errors: [{ msg: 'Validation failed due to a nunknown error' }],
+      })
+    })
+  })
+
+  describe('updateCard', () => {
+    it('calls API with card identifier', async () => {
+      mockUpdateCard.mockResolvedValue(undefined)
+
+      const { updateCard } = usePromotions()
+      await updateCard('card-xyz')
+
+      expect(mockUpdateCard).toHaveBeenCalledWith('card-xyz')
+    })
+
+    it('rethrows on API error', async () => {
+      const error = new Error('Update failed')
+      mockUpdateCard.mockRejectedValue(error)
+
+      const { updateCard } = usePromotions()
+      await expect(updateCard('card-xyz')).rejects.toThrow('Update failed')
+    })
+  })
+})

+import { renderHook, act } from '@testing-library/react'
+
+import { useRouteRedirection } from '../useRouteRedirection'
+import { NavigationManager } from '@/app/utils/navigation'
+
+jest.mock('@/app/providers/GeneralConfigProvider', () => ({
+  useGeneralConfig: jest.fn(),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/hooks/api/commerces/useCommerces', () => ({
+  useCommerces: jest.fn(),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/utils/navigation', () => ({
+  NavigationManager: {
+    replace: jest.fn(),
+    navigate: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useRouteRedirection (extra coverage)', () => {
+  const useGeneralConfigMock =
+    require('@/app/providers/GeneralConfigProvider')
+      .useGeneralConfig as jest.Mock
+  const useCommercesMock =
+    require('@/app/hooks/api/commerces/useCommerces').useCommerces as jest.Mock
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    useGeneralConfigMock.mockReturnValue({
+      config: {
+        contextBarMerchantSelected: 'company-1:commerce-1',
+      },
+    })
+    useCommercesMock.mockReturnValue({
+      commerce: {
+        id: 'commerce-2',
+        companyId: 'company-2',
+      },
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('navigates to dynamic route with query params when resolvable', async () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    let response = false
+    await act(async () => {
+      response = await result.current.navigateToDynamicRoute('/l', {
+        source: 'test',
+      })
+    })
+
+    expect(response).toBe(true)
+    expect(NavigationManager.replace).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      '/(drawer)/(tabs)/company/company-1/commerce/commerce-1/presencia-digital/datos-de-publicacion?source=test',
+    )
+  })
+
+  it('falls back when required params cannot be resolved', async () => {
+    useGeneralConfigMock.mockReturnValue({
+      config: {
+        contextBarMerchantSelected: '',
+      },
+    })
+    useCommercesMock.mockReturnValue({
+      commerce: undefined,
+    })
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    let response = true
+    await act(async () => {
+      response = await result.current.navigateToDynamicRoute('/reviews')
+    })
+
+    expect(response).toBe(false)
+    expect(NavigationManager.replace).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/')
+  })
+
+  it('returns false when mapping does not exist', async () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    let response = true
+    await act(async () => {
+      response = await result.current.navigateToDynamicRoute('/does-not-exist')
+    })
+
+    expect(response).toBe(false)
+    expect(NavigationManager.replace).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
+  })
+
+  it('canResolveRoute returns false when params are unavailable', () => {
+    useGeneralConfigMock.mockReturnValue({
+      config: {
+        contextBarMerchantSelected: '',
+      },
+    })
+    useCommercesMock.mockReturnValue({
+      commerce: undefined,
+    })
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useRouteRedirection())
+
+    expect(result.current.canResolveRoute('/l')).toBe(false)
+  })
+})

+import { renderHook } from '@testing-library/react-native'
+import useSWR from 'swr'
+
+import { useBilling } from './useBilling'
+import { billingApi } from '../../../services/api/billing'
+
+jest.mock('swr')
+
+jest.mock('../../../services/api/billing', () => ({
+  billingApi: {
+    getBilling: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useBilling (extra coverage)', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    ;(useSWR as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      data: [],
+      error: undefined,
+      isLoading: false,
+      mutate: jest.fn(),
+    })
+  })
+
+  it('calls billing API for each valid merchant id in fetcher', async () => {
+    ;(billingApi.getBilling as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue([{ invoiceId: '1' }])

+    renderHook(() =>

+        'merchant-1',
+        undefined,
+        'merchant-2',
+      ]),
+    )
+
+    const fetcher = (useSWR as jest.Mock).mock.calls[0][1]
+    await fetcher()
+
+    expect(billingApi.getBilling).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)
+    expect(billingApi.getBilling).toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(
+      1,
+      '2024-01-01',
+      '2024-01-31',

+      1,
+      10,
+    )
+    expect(billingApi.getBilling).toHaveBeenNthCalledWith(
+      2,
+      '2024-01-01',

+      10,

+import { renderHook, act } from '@testing-library/react-native'

+
+import { useCommerces } from './useCommerces'
+import { commerceApi } from '@/app/services/api/commerces/api'

+jest.mock('swr')

+}))
+

+    translations: {
+      ERROR_UPDATING_PLACES: 'Error updating places',

+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/providers/GeneralConfigProvider', () => ({
+  useGeneralConfig: jest.fn(() => ({
+    config: {
+      contextBarMerchantSelected: 'company-1:commerce-1',
+    },
+  })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/api/commerces/api', () => ({
+  commerceApi: {
+    getCommerces: jest.fn(),
+    updateCommerce: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/shared/toast', () => ({
+  toast: {
+    show: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useCommerces updateCommerce', () => {
+  const mutate = jest.fn()
+
+  const swrData = {
+    commerces: [
+      { id: 'commerce-1', companyId: 'company-1', name: 'One' },
+      { id: 'commerce-2', companyId: 'company-1', name: 'Two' },
+    ],
+    companies: [{ id: 'company-1', name: 'Company' }],
+  }
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    ;(useSWR as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      data: swrData,
+      error: undefined,
+      isLoading: false,
+      mutate,
+    })
+    mutate.mockResolvedValue(swrData)
+    ;(commerceApi.updateCommerce as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(undefined)
+  })
+
+  it('computes selectedMerchant and commerce from context/search params', () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    expect(result.current.selectedMerchant?.id).toBe('commerce-1')
+    expect(result.current.commerce?.id).toBe('commerce-2')
+  })
+
+  it('updates commerce with object payload using optimistic update + revalidate', async () => {
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.updateCommerce('commerce-1', { name: 'Updated' })
+    })
+
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.any(Function), false)
+    expect(commerceApi.updateCommerce).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      'commerce-1',
+      'merchant',
+      { name: 'Updated' },
+    )
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)
+  })
+
+  it('updates commerce with FormData and executes onSuccess callback', async () => {
+    const onSuccess = jest.fn()
+    const formData = new FormData()
+    formData.append('file', 'avatar')
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.updateCommerce('commerce-2', formData, onSuccess)
+    })
+
+    expect(commerceApi.updateCommerce).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
+      'commerce-2',
+      'merchant',
+      formData,
+    )
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
+    expect(onSuccess).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
+  })
+
+  it('restores previous data and shows toast when update fails', async () => {
+    ;(commerceApi.updateCommerce as jest.Mock).mockRejectedValue(
+      new Error('boom'),
+    )
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useCommerces())
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.updateCommerce('commerce-1', { name: 'Broken' })
+    })
+
+    expect(toast.show).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
+      title: 'Error updating places',
+      type: 'error',
+    })
+    expect(mutate).toHaveBeenCalledWith(swrData, false)
+  })
+})

+import { act, renderHook } from '@testing-library/react-native'
+

+import { useServices } from '../services/useServices'
+
+jest.mock('@/app/providers/LanguageProvider', () => ({
+  useLanguage: jest.fn(() => ({ selectedLang: 'es-ES' })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('expo-router', () => ({
+  useGlobalSearchParams: jest.fn(() => ({ company_id: 'company-1' })),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('../services/useServices')
+
+jest.mock('swr', () => ({
+  __esModule: true,
+  default: jest.fn(),
+}))
+
+jest.mock('@/app/services/api/services', () => ({
+  servicesApi: {
+    getServiceMetricoolUrl: jest.fn(),
+    activateMetricool: jest.fn(),
+  },
+}))
+
+describe('useMetricool activate flow', () => {
+  const useSWRMock = require('swr').default as jest.Mock
+  const mutateUrl = jest.fn()
+  const mutateServices = jest.fn()
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    jest.clearAllMocks()
+    jest.useFakeTimers()
+
+    ;(servicesApi.getServiceMetricoolUrl as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue({
+      loginUrl: 'https://metricool.example.com/login',
+    })
+    ;(servicesApi.activateMetricool as jest.Mock).mockResolvedValue(undefined)
+
+    useSWRMock.mockReturnValue({
+      data: 'https://metricool.example.com/login',
+      isLoading: false,
+      error: undefined,
+      mutate: mutateUrl,
+    })
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    jest.useRealTimers()
+  })
+
+  it('activates metricool and refreshes url when account id exists', async () => {
+    ;(useServices as jest.Mock).mockReturnValue({
+      services: [
+        {
+          id: 'service-1',
+          data: { metricoolAccountId: 'account-1' },
+        },
+      ],
+      mutate: mutateServices,
+    })
+
+    const { result } = renderHook(() => useMetricool('service-1', 'inbox'))
+
+    await act(async () => {
+      await result.current.activate()
+    })
+
+    expect(servicesApi.activateMetricool).toHaveBeenCalledWith('service-1')
+    expect(mutateServices).toHaveBeenCalled()
+    expect(mutateUrl).toHaveBeenCalled()
+    expect(result.current.isActivating).toBe(false)
+  })
+
+})
